description_long: This is a single-story log cabin with a rectangular footprint, constructed primarily from horizontally stacked wooden logs and set on a stone or concrete foundation. The cabin features a prominent gable roof with a dark, matte finish and two visible chimneys, suggesting the presence of fireplaces or stoves inside. Large windows are present along one of the longer facades, providing ample natural light and a view to the outside, while the opposite side has minimal fenestration. The ends of the cabin display the characteristic interlocking log corners, enhancing its rustic appearance. A covered porch extends from one of the shorter sides, supported by sturdy wooden posts and providing a sheltered entryway. The structure is surrounded by a circular patch of grass, indicating it is designed to be placed in a natural or park-like urban setting.
description: A rustic log cabin with a dark gable roof, large windows on one side, and a covered porch supported by wooden posts. The structure sits on a stone foundation and is surrounded by grass.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the side with large windows and a visible stone foundation, highlighting the log construction and the overhanging roof.
description_view_1: This view shows the gable end with the covered porch, wooden posts, and the interlocking log corners, as well as the roof's overhang.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, featuring minimal windows and a solid log wall, with the stone foundation running along the base.
description_view_3: The other gable end is shown, with the porch area and entryway clearly visible, as well as the roof's structure and support beams.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a moderate pitch, extended overhangs, and two chimneys; covered in dark, matte roofing material
attribute_window_arrangement: One long side features a row of large, rectangular windows arranged asymmetrically, while the opposite side has minimal or no windows; no windows are visible on the gable ends
attribute_door_placement: A single exterior door is located on one gable end, integrated into the covered porch area and centered between the supporting posts
